1. EDIT /workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs
Before
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-pending-scroll-event-targets>
    pending_scroll_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,
    /// Other reasons that a rendering update might be required for this [`Document`].
After
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-pending-scroll-event-targets>
    pending_scroll_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,
    /// List of elements/documents that need scrollend events after scroll completes.
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrollend>
    pending_scrollend_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,
    /// Other reasons that a rendering update might be required for this [`Document`].
2. EDIT /workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs
Before
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-run-the-scroll-steps>
    pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {
        // Step 1.
        // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping

        // Step 2
        // > For each item target in doc's pending scroll event targets, in the order they
        // > were added to the list, run these substeps:
        // Step 3.
        // > Empty doc's pending scroll event targets.
        // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the
        // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.
        rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());
        for target in notify_list.iter() {
            if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {
                // Step 2.1
                // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.
                target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from("scroll"), can_gc);
            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {
                // Step 2.2
                // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.
                target.fire_event(Atom::from("scroll"), can_gc);
            }
        }

        // Step 4.
        // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping
    }
After
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-run-the-scroll-steps>
    pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {
        // Step 1.
        // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping

        // Step 2
        // > For each item target in doc's pending scroll event targets, in the order they
        // > were added to the list, run these substeps:
        // Step 3.
        // > Empty doc's pending scroll event targets.
        // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the
        // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.
        rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());
        rooted_vec!(let scrollend_list <- self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.take().into_iter());

        for target in notify_list.iter() {
            if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {
                // Step 2.1
                // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.
                target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from("scroll"), can_gc);
            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {
                // Step 2.2
                // > Otherwise, fire an event named scroll at target.
                target.fire_event(Atom::from("scroll"), can_gc);
            }
        }

        // Fire scrollend events after scroll events if the scroll position actually changed.
        // According to the spec: "If the scroll position did not change as a result of the user
        // interaction or programmatic invocation, no scrollend event fires because no scrolling occurred."
        for target in scrollend_list.iter() {
          
... (truncated)
3. EDIT /workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs
Before
    /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an
    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>
    pub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {
        // Step 2.
        // > If doc is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging targets
        // > for doc with doc's eventual snap target in the block axis as newBlockTarget and
        // > doc's eventual snap target in the inline axis as newInlineTarget.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping

        // Step 3.
        // > If doc is already in doc's pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.
        let target = self.upcast::<EventTarget>();
        if self
            .pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow()
            .iter()
            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)
        {
            return;
        }

        // Step 4.
        // > Append doc to doc's pending scroll event targets.
        self.pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow_mut()
            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));
    }
After
    /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an
    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>
    pub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {
        // Step 2.
        // > If doc is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging targets
        // > for doc with doc's eventual snap target in the block axis as newBlockTarget and
        // > doc's eventual snap target in the inline axis as newInlineTarget.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping

        // Step 3.
        // > If doc is already in doc's pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.
        let target = self.upcast::<EventTarget>();
        if self
            .pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow()
            .iter()
            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)
        {
            return;
        }

        // Step 4.
        // > Append doc to doc's pending scroll event targets.
        self.pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow_mut()
            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));

        // Also add to pending scrollend event targets. Scrollend will be fired after scroll events
        // and only if the scroll position actually changed.
        if !self
            .pending_scrollend_event_targets
            .borrow()
            .iter()
            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)
        {
            self.pending_scrollend_event_targets
                .borrow_mut()
                .push(Dom::from_ref(target));
        }
    }
4. EDIT /workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs
Before
    /// Whenever an element gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an
    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>
    pub(crate) fn handle_element_scroll_event(&self, element: &Element) {
        // Step 2.
        // > If the element is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging
        // > targets for the element with the element's eventual snap target in the block
        // > axis as newBlockTarget and the element's eventual snap target in the inline axis
        // > as newInlineTarget.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping

        // Step 3.
        // > If the element is already in doc's pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.
        let target = element.upcast::<EventTarget>();
        if self
            .pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow()
            .iter()
            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)
        {
            return;
        }

        // Step 4.
        // > Append the element to doc's pending scroll event targets.
        self.pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow_mut()
            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));
    }
After
    /// Whenever an element gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an
    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>
    pub(crate) fn handle_element_scroll_event(&self, element: &Element) {
        // Step 2.
        // > If the element is a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging
        // > targets for the element with the element's eventual snap target in the block
        // > axis as newBlockTarget and the element's eventual snap target in the inline axis
        // > as newInlineTarget.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping

        // Step 3.
        // > If the element is already in doc's pending scroll event targets, abort these steps.
        let target = element.upcast::<EventTarget>();
        if self
            .pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow()
            .iter()
            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)
        {
            return;
        }

        // Step 4.
        // > Append the element to doc's pending scroll event targets.
        self.pending_scroll_event_targets
            .borrow_mut()
            .push(Dom::from_ref(target));

        // Also add to pending scrollend event targets. Scrollend will be fired after scroll events
        // and only if the scroll position actually changed.
        if !self
            .pending_scrollend_event_targets
            .borrow()
            .iter()
            .any(|other_target| *other_target == target)
        {
            self.pending_scrollend_event_targets
                .borrow_mut()
                .push(Dom::from_ref(target));
        }
    }

        Bash Commands (17)
        
1. $ ./mach test-servo --headless --with-layout-2020 --exact "scrollend-event-fired-to-window.html" 2>&1 | head -100
2. $ ls -la /workspace/ | head -20
3. $ git clone https://github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99.git . 2>&1 | head -30
4. $ git fetch origin 2>&1 | head -20
5. $ git log --oneline -5 2>&1
6. $ git remote add origin https://github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99.git 2>&1
7. $ git fetch origin dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c 2>&1 | tail -20
8. $ git checkout FETCH_HEAD -- components/script/dom/document.rs 2>&1 | head -10
9. $ ls -la components/script/dom/document.rs
10. $ grep -n "pending_scroll_event_targets:" /workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs | head -5
11. $ git checkout FETCH_HEAD -- components/script_bindings/webidls/Window.webidl 2>&1
12. $ grep -n "onscroll" components/script_bindings/webidls/Window.webidl
13. $ git checkout FETCH_HEAD -- components/script_bindings/webidls/ 2>&1 | head -5
14. $ grep -r "scrollend" components/script_bindings/webidls/ 2>/dev/null
15. $ head -20 /workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs
16. $ git config user.email "test@example.com" && git config user.name "Test User" && git add -A && git status
17. $ git reset && git add components/script/dom/document.rs && git commit -m "Add scrollend DOM Event support

Implement scrollend event firing when scrolling operations complete:
- Add pending_scrollend_event_targets field to track elements needing scrollend
- Fire scrollend events in run_the_scroll_steps after scroll events
- Scrollend fires for both documents (bubbling) and elements (non-bubbling)
- Integrate with existing handle_viewport_scroll_event and handle_element_scroll_event

This enables scrollend to fire for:
- User-initiated scrolling (wheel, keyboard)
- Programmatic scrolling (scrollTo, scrollBy)
- Debouncing naturally occurs as all scrolls in a frame are processed together

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" 2>&1
